sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data available in sources

special_mechanics.name: Helicopter Movement
special_mechanics.description: Primary gameplay involves piloting the Mohican helicopter designed for speed and combat. Alternative larger Blackhawke model available for rescue missions. Finite fuel depletion system requiring fuel barrel collection.

physics_values.fuel_system: finite fuel depleted per level progression
physics_values.crash_condition: fuel depletion results in loss of life

special_mechanics.name: On-Foot Missions
special_mechanics.description: Player leaves helicopter at certain points to enter internal environments armed with rifle and missile launcher

special_mechanics.name: Armor/Damage System
special_mechanics.description: Limited armor depleted by enemy fire. Armor replenished via power-ups or by airlifting rescued friendlies/captives to landing zone

special_mechanics.name: Weapon System
special_mechanics.description: Helicopter armed with machine guns, Hydra rockets, and Hellfire missiles. More powerful weapons carry fewer ammunition units

physics_values.ammo_limitation: inverse relationship between weapon power and ammunition capacity

tier_notes: Tier 2: Empirical/qualitative data only. Sources document gameplay mechanics and vehicle descriptions but contain no explicit movement physics parameters, sub-pixel system details, gravity values, or jump mechanics. Game is helicopter-based shoot 'em up with on-foot segments rather than traditional platformer physics.
sources_used: wikipedia - Urban Strike article (gameplay mechanics, vehicle types, level structure)
tcrf - Urban Strike (SNES) page (development info, unused content)
